description: RT @alexdolbun: @clanker_world @BNBCHAIN @BitgetWallet Hey Mr. @0xDeployer, Mr. @igoryuzo check this amazing #BNB functionality by our $BNK… (via Twitter https://twitter.com/alexdolbun/status/2032771857754239040)

Converts any PNG/JPG/WebP image into a 3D depth-displaced mesh using DepthAnything-Small AI model via Xenova/transformers.js. No server, no API keys, no backend — everything runs locally in your browser with WebAssembly + ONNX Runtime.  Features: - AI depth estimation (DepthAnything-Small ~24MB, auto-cached after first use) - Fallback luminance heuristic if AI unavailable - Adjustable resolution, depth scale, and smoothing - Optional closed back face, side walls, texture overlay - Wireframe mode, auto-rotate, invert depth - Live Three.js r128 WebGL preview with orbit/pan/zoom controls - Export as .OBJ + .MTL + texture.png in a .ZIP (open in Blender or 3dviewer.net)  Dependencies (all public CDN, no install): - Three.js r128 - @xenova/transformers 2.17.2 - JSZip 3.10.1  Drop an image → AI generates depth map → 3D mesh renders instantly.
